senses.definition: a heavy and difficult situation or responsibility that someone has to deal with

word_list.pronunciation: bˈæt biː ˌɑːn ɐ stˈɪki wˈɪkɪt

senses.definition: to be dealing with a critical or extremely difficult situation

examples.x: I knew I was batting on a sticky wicket when the boss saw me kissing his daughter at the cinema.
Trying to navigate the complex world of international trade, the negotiators were on a sticky wicket.
In the competitive job market, finding a position that matches your qualifications can be like batting on a sticky wicket.

senses.definition: to be expecting something difficult or undesirable to happen

examples.x: When they heard the thunder, they knew they were in for a stormy evening.
With his recent performance at work, he believed he was in for a promotion.
After hearing about the new project's complexity, she realized she was in for a lot of hard work.
With the early symptoms of a cold, he understood he was in for a few days of feeling unwell.
Upon receiving the news of the unexpected visit, they knew they were in for a surprise.

senses.definition: to get ready for trouble, difficulty, or danger

examples.x: With a hurricane approaching, the residents were advised to batten down the hatches and prepare for the worst.
Before the major product launch, the team needed to batten down the hatches and ensure everything was in order.
Knowing that a challenging project was ahead, they decided to batten down the hatches and create a detailed plan.
In the face of an economic downturn, the company needed to batten down the hatches to weather the financial storm.
When faced with a family crisis, they knew it was time to batten down the hatches and support each other.

senses.definition: said to man as a way of asking them to behave less emotional, more though, or more responsible in face of difficulties

examples.x: Stop crying over losing your cat and be a man already!
You need to be a man and accept the punishment coming to you.
When he hesitated to confront the issue, his friends told him to be a man and address the problem.
In the face of adversity, his father encouraged him to be a man and confront his fears.
When she was tempted to give up, her coach urged her to be a man and finish the race.

senses.definition: to get into a confrontation with a person or thing that is more powerful than what one assumed

examples.x: It looks like we caught a Tartar when we tried to muscle their store out of the area.
He thought the assignment would be easy, but it turned out to be a real challenge, like trying to catch a Tartar.
The young chess champion didn't realize he would catch a Tartar when he challenged the seasoned grandmaster.
Dealing with the demanding client proved to be catching a Tartar; they had high expectations and a sharp eye for detail.

senses.definition: a miserable life filled with difficulties and misfortune

examples.x: Working two jobs and barely having time for oneself can feel like living a dog's life.
Growing up in poverty, he experienced a dog's life, struggling for basic necessities.
Taking care of her ailing parents and her own family made her feel like she was living a dog's life.
During the peak of the recession, many people were forced to live a dog's life, trying to make ends meet.
The relentless demands of her high-stress job sometimes made her feel as if she had a dog's life.

senses.definition: to experience something that is extremely painful or difficult, particularly for a long time

examples.x: During the war, the soldiers went through hell, facing constant danger and hardship.
"After the loss of her loved one, she felt like she was going through hell, struggling with grief and sadness.
The athlete trained rigorously for the competition, going through hell to achieve peak performance.
Many families went through hell during the economic recession, facing unemployment and financial struggles.
He had to go through hell to overcome his addiction and rebuild his life.

senses.definition: in trouble or a very difficult situation

examples.x: After making a costly mistake at work, he found himself in deep water with his superiors.
The company's financial troubles left it in deep water, struggling to stay afloat.
Getting caught in a web of deceit and betrayal, he realized he was in deep water.
The legal issues that arose from the contract dispute put the business in deep water, leading to legal battles and financial losses.

senses.definition: a situation or state that is very undesirable or difficult

examples.x: The sudden downpour during the picnic made for a pretty pass, with everyone scrambling for cover.
After a series of unfortunate events, the project was in a pretty pass, and the team had to work hard to get back on track.
Discovering the extent of the financial mismanagement put the organization in a pretty pass, necessitating immediate action.
The city's traffic congestion had reached a pretty pass, causing daily delays for commuters.
The family's disagreement over the inheritance had led to a pretty pass, straining their relationships.

senses.definition: a worrisome or difficult responsibility or undertaking that is a burden to one

examples.x: After his promotion, he felt the weight of the world on his shoulders as he assumed greater responsibilities at work.
When dealing with her family's financial troubles, she often felt like she was carrying the weight of the world.
The mayor faced a crisis that made it seem as if he had the weight of the world on his shoulders.
The young activist took on environmental causes and felt the weight of the world in her mission to make a difference.
Coping with the illness of a loved one can make one feel as if they have the weight of the world on their shoulders.

senses.definition: (particularly of an event or a difficult situation) to cause a lot of suffering to someone and make them look or feel much older

examples.x: The stress of his demanding job has put years on him; he looks much older than his actual age.
The responsibility of caring for her ailing parents has put years on her, but it has also made her stronger.
The constant financial struggles have put years on them; they look worn out from the constant worry.
Surviving a life-threatening illness has put years on him, but it has also given him a greater appreciation for life.

senses.definition: (of a problem or unpleasant situation) to appear or become noticeable often unexpectedly

examples.x: Fundamentalist extremism has been raising its head all around the world over the last couple years.
After years of peace, political unrest raised its ugly head in the region, causing concern among the population.
Just when they thought the financial crisis was over, economic instability reared its head, affecting businesses and individuals once again.
The long-standing issue of discrimination raised its ugly head when a controversial policy change was proposed.
Despite their efforts to resolve the conflict, the dispute between the neighbors reared its head with renewed intensity.

senses.definition: (of a person) placed in a difficult situation with little or no resources or help

examples.x: The bottom line was that I was high and dry.
The inadequate supplies of vaccine left many people high and dry when the flu season arrived.
When her friends abandoned her during the hike, she was left high and dry in the wilderness.
The sudden closure of the company left many employees high and dry without jobs or a source of income.
His decision to invest all his savings in a risky venture left him high and dry when it failed.

word_list.word: in the eye of the storm

senses.definition: used to refer to being in the middle of a chaotic or controversial situation while maintaining a sense of control

examples.x: The Prime Minister was often in the eye of the storm during the debate on whether or not to intervene in Syria.
Despite the chaos of the corporate merger, the CEO remained in the eye of the storm, making strategic decisions.
As the family faced a crisis, the mother kept everyone in the eye of the storm grounded and focused on solutions.

senses.definition: used to say that a period of happiness, enjoyment, etc. has come to an end and one's life is going to get back to the state it previously was, if not worse
a day that is full of incidents and misfortunes

examples.x: They had a bad hair day on their vacation, encountering unexpected weather, canceled plans, and lost luggage.
Today feels like a bad hair day for him, with one mishap after another, from spilling coffee on his shirt to missing the bus.

senses.definition: in an inevitable confrontation with an unpleasant or difficult situation

examples.x: Eventually, he came face to face with discrimination again.
They knew they had to meet face to face with their financial struggles and come up with a plan to overcome them.

senses.definition: (of situations, moods, or relationships) to become unpleasant or unsuccessful

examples.x: Unfortunately, their friendship started to go sour after a series of misunderstandings.
The collaboration between the two teams began to go sour due to conflicting interests.
Their romantic relationship went sour when they realized they had different long-term goals.
Things started to go sour between the business partners when financial issues arose.
His relationship with colleagues went sour after a disagreement over a project.
The business deal began to go sour when unexpected challenges arose.
The atmosphere at the family gathering quickly went sour due to heated arguments.
His excitement about the new job quickly went sour when he realized the stressful workload.
The celebration started to go sour when technical difficulties disrupted the event.
The once-promising project started to go sour as deadlines were repeatedly missed.
